name: Epitalon
subtitle: Synthetic Pineal Tetrapeptide | Telomerase Activator

modifications: Synthetic analog of natural pineal gland epithalamin

overview: Epitalon is a synthetic tetrapeptide developed by Russian scientist Vladimir Khavinson with 35+ years of research. It activates telomerase at extremely low concentrations to maintain telomere length, stimulates melatonin production, and modulates gene expression through epigenetic mechanisms.
mechanism: Activates telomerase at extremely low concentrations (10⁻¹⁷ to 10⁻¹⁵ M) to maintain telomere length, stimulates melatonin production from the pineal gland, and modulates gene expression through epigenetic mechanisms.
keyBenefits: Telomere extension and cellular replicative lifespan extension
Potential lifespan extension (25-33% in animal studies)
Short cycle duration with long-lasting effects

items.name: Telomere Extension
items.description: Activates telomerase at microgram doses to extend telomeres and cellular replicative lifespan.

items.name: Cellular Rejuvenation
items.description: Reverses aging markers and improves mitochondrial function at the cellular level.

items.name: Lifespan Extension
items.description: Animal studies show 25-33% lifespan increases using 30-40mcg/kg doses.

items.name: Pineal Function Restoration
items.description: Restores pineal gland function and circadian melatonin rhythm in elderly.

items.name: Neurogenesis Support
items.description: Supports new neuron formation at nanogram concentrations.

items.name: Retinal Protection
items.description: 90% response rate for retinal degeneration at 5mcg per eye dosing.

items.name: Thymus Regeneration
items.description: Supports thymus gland regeneration for improved immune function.

items.name: Immune Surveillance
items.description: Enhanced immune surveillance and countering of immunosenescence.

deliveryMethods.overview: Subcutaneous injection is the primary and most researched route. Short cycles of 10-20 days with 4-6 month breaks between cycles.

materials: Insulin syringes (0.3mL for accurate microgram dosing)
steps: Clean vial tops with alcohol swabs
Inject water slowly at vial side wall
Gently swirl - do not shake vigorously
Solution should be clear and colorless
Use 0.3mL insulin syringes for accurate microgram dosing

keyBenefits: Abdomen, thigh, or upper arm injection sites

deliveryMethods.overview: Intranasal spray offers non-invasive administration with potentially faster absorption, though less research data available.
Oral administration has reduced bioavailability due to digestive breakdown. Less predictable effects compared to injectable.

interactions.notes: Both work through pineal pathways; may enhance circadian rhythm effects.
Enhanced immunomodulation - commonly combined in Russian protocols.
Different axis activation; no known interactions.
Both are neuroprotective; monitor for cumulative effects.
Telomerase activation may theoretically affect malignancy; avoid with active cancer.

timeline.effects: Better sleep quality, vivid dreams (melatonin effect)
Skin quality improvements, enhanced wound healing
Laboratory markers may show telomere changes
Cumulative anti-aging benefits with repeated cycles

common: Generally well-tolerated with minimal side effects
monitoring: Optional: telomere length testing before/after cycles

warning: Many sources recommend 5-10mg based on translation errors

references.title: Telomerase Activation Study
references.keyFindings: Human fibroblasts showed extended replicative lifespan (34→44+ passages) at 0.1μM concentration while maintaining telomere length comparable to young cells.

references.title: Russian Clinical Trials
references.keyFindings: Long-term observations showed significantly lower all-cause mortality in elderly subjects receiving periodic epithalamin courses with improved cardiovascular and immune parameters.

references.title: Pineal Function Study
references.keyFindings: Elderly patients showed restoration of circadian melatonin rhythm and improved sleep/emotional stability using low-dose synthetic treatment.

references.title: Retinal Degeneration Study
references.keyFindings: AMD patients achieved 90% positive response with only 5mcg per eye daily dosing.

references.title: Lifespan Extension Study
references.keyFindings: Aging mice using 30-40mcg/kg showed 25% lifespan increase and reduced chromosomal aberrations.

references.title: Neurogenesis Study
references.keyFindings: Human stem cells showed 1.6-1.8 fold upregulation of neurogenic markers at extremely low concentrations (0.01mcg/mL).

protocolVariants.name: Research-Based Low Dose
protocolVariants.philosophy: Use doses validated in peer-reviewed human research
protocolVariants.description: Based on actual human clinical trials, including a study of 75 women using 0.5mg/day sublingually for 20 days. Animal studies typically used 0.1-10mcg/mouse/day. This approach prioritizes evidence-based dosing over anecdotal community protocols.
keyDifferences: Supported by peer-reviewed human clinical data
Aligns with research showing microgram-level efficacy

protocolVariants.name: High-Dose Community Protocol
protocolVariants.philosophy: Higher doses for potentially stronger effects
protocolVariants.description: Widely circulated protocol recommending 5-10mg daily injections. This dosing lacks peer-reviewed human clinical data and may stem from translation errors of Russian research or extrapolation from other peptides. No published studies validate this dose range in humans.
keyDifferences: No peer-reviewed human data at these doses
Unknown if higher doses provide additional benefit
May have originated from translation errors

quickStats.storage: Lyophilized: 2-8°C refrigerated; Reconstituted: 2-8°C refrigerated, use within 30 days
